task_completion.description: Open the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app's main screen or interface is visible.
'Vemurafenib' is visible in the selected cancer drugs list or search bar within the app.
'Quetiapine' is visible in the selected co-medications list or search bar within the app.

task_completion.description: View the interaction result between Vemurafenib and Quetiapine.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The interaction result screen is displayed, showing the interaction between Vemurafenib and Quetiapine.

task_completion.description: Open the required text file for editing or viewing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal is open, showing the file path /sdcard/tasks/vemurafenib_quetiapine_check.txt.

task_completion.description: Document the cancer drug and co-medication names in the text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains 'Vemurafenib' on Line 1 and 'Quetiapine' on Line 2.

task_completion.description: Document the interaction color and clinical risk in the text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains 'Yellow' on Line 3 and a snippet mentioning CYP3A4 induction or reduced Quetiapine efficacy on Line 4.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the genuine app interface being navigated, not just a browser or text file with the answers.

integrity.description: The agent did not hallucinate the expected 'QT' or 'cardiac' risk from the task title.
integrity.visual_evidence: The documented risk in the text file matches the actual app output (CYP3A4 induction/reduced efficacy) and does not mention QT interval or cardiac risks.

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI to find the interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are screenshots showing the app's graphical interface being used to search and view the interaction, rather than using terminal commands to query the API directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The actual interaction between Vemurafenib and Quetiapine in the app is 'Yellow' (Potential Weak Interaction). The clinical recommendation details CYP3A4 induction risks and reduced Quetiapine efficacy. It does NOT mention 'QT', 'interval', or 'cardiac' risks, despite the task title. The output file must be at /sdcard/tasks/vemurafenib_quetiapine_check.txt.
